title: Cryo-Expansion Microscopy of C. elegans and Tardigrades
author: Matthew Lycas
materials: Degas both cryoprotectant solutions prior to use by placing them following mixing in a cell media sterilization flask connected to a vacuum. Run the vacuum until no more bubbles emerge from the solution
Monitor specimen release under the dissectionmicroscope:
For NHS ester total protein staining:
Re-expand gel in deionized water for 1 hour.
Re-expand in deionized water until fully expanded.

steps.title: Transfer C
steps.instruction: Transfer C. elegans from plates into a well of a 9-well borosilicate spot plate using a worm pick or P200 pipette. Pool sufficient animals for multiple HPF runs.

steps.title: Collect moss from tree bark and...
steps.instruction: Collect moss from tree bark and place in a 60 mmPetri dish.

steps.title: Add deionized water until the moss is barely submerged
steps.instruction: Add deionized water until the moss is barely submerged.

steps.title: Incubate at room temperature for 45...
steps.instruction: Incubate at room temperature for 45 minutes toallow tardigrades to egress from the moss into the water.

steps.title: Under a stereodissection microscope, identify tardigrades...
steps.instruction: Under a stereodissection microscope, identify tardigrades in the water film and collect individually using a P200 pipette. Transfer to a well of a 9-well spot plate.

steps.title: In a separate well of the...
steps.instruction: In a separate well of the spot plate, place 1 mL of the appropriate cryoprotectant solution: 20% FBS in deionized water (tardigrades) or 20% dextran in 0.1 M PB (C. elegans).

steps.title: Pre-cool the Leica EM ICE high-pressure...
steps.instruction: Pre-cool the Leica EM ICE high-pressure freezer by filling the internal dewar with LN2 using a large storage dewar. Allow the machine to equilibrate to LN2 temperature (~20 minutes). Fill the HPF samplecollection dewar with LN2 and insert into the freezer chamber prior to beginning runs.

steps.title: Mark the shallow face of all...
steps.instruction: Mark the shallow face of all Type A planchets (the 0.1 mm depression face) with a permanent marker. This allows orientation identification post-freezing under LN2.

steps.title: Immerse the flat face of a...
steps.instruction: Immerse the flat face of a Type B planchet in anhydrous hexadecane and blot briefly then place on a post-it note to leave a thin, even film. This facilitates the future separation of the planchets. Planchets used for the HPF preparation

steps.title: Assemble the loading cartridge in the HPF machine
steps.instruction: Assemble the loading cartridge in the HPF machine. Place the Type A planchet into the cartridge holder with the 0.2 mm depression facing upward.

steps.title: Using a P2 pipette set to...
steps.instruction: Using a P2 pipette set to 2 µL, transfer as many specimens as possible from the cryoprotectant well and dispense the 2 µL volume directly into the 0.2 mm depression of the Type A planchet. Work quickly, this is not a physiologically normal environment for the specimen.

steps.title: Seat the hexadecane-coated flat face of...
steps.instruction: Seat the hexadecane-coated flat face of the Type B planchet on top to sandwich the sample. Initiate the HPF run by closing the loading lever. The specimen will be frozen under ~2,000 bar within milliseconds.

steps.title: The frozen planchet assembly is ejected...
steps.instruction: The frozen planchet assembly is ejected and deposited into the LN2-filled collection reservoir within the machine.

steps.title: Repeat Steps the freezing sample steps...
steps.instruction: Repeat Steps the freezing sample steps for a total of 10 HPF runs.

steps.title: After completing all runs, retrieve the...
steps.instruction: After completing all runs, retrieve the planchet assemblies from the collection dewar under LN2. Identify the Type A planchet (marked on the 0.1 mm face) and separate. Setup for removing the planchets from spaces under cryo conditions. In the image these are currently at room temperature. For their use, the container is filled with liquid nitrogen and the planchet assembaly

steps.title: Perform one blank (empty) run at...
steps.instruction: Perform one blank (empty) run at the start of the HPF session to purge the system and verify function

steps.title: Add 50 mL of anhydrous acetone...
steps.instruction: Add 50 mL of anhydrous acetone to an 80 mL plastic beaker. Cover with aluminum foil.

steps.title: Submerge the beaker in a LN2...
steps.instruction: Submerge the beaker in a LN2 bath until the acetone freezes solid. Continue cooling until the beaker reaches LN2 temperature (allow several minutes).

steps.title: Under LN2 vapor, transfer the HPF...
steps.instruction: Under LN2 vapor, transfer the HPF planchet assemblies onto the surface of the frozen acetone block inside the beaker. To do this, bring the planchets in a plastic carrier into liquid nitrogen with the beaker of frozen acetone (covered with aluminium foil to reduce water condensing and in the beaker). Then remove the foil from the acetone beaker, and with fine forceps, transfer each planchet to the surface of the acetone. Once all the planchets are transfered, re-cover the acetone beaker with foil. Transfer the planchets with the samples to the covered beaker with frozen acetone. The planchets are in the plastic carrier on the left side. The beaker of acetone is on the right, covered with foil. The planchets from each well of the plastic carrier need to be placed on the surface of the frozen acetone, and finally the beaker is recovered with foil and placed in the -80°C freezer.

steps.title: Transfer the covered beaker to a -80°C freezer
steps.instruction: Transfer the covered beaker to a -80°C freezer. Maintain at -80°C for 7 days (1 week). During this time, acetone slowly permeates the sample as a substitution medium, replacing vitreous water whilepreserving ultrastructure.

steps.title: Remove the beaker from -80°C and...
steps.instruction: Remove the beaker from -80°C and allow to warm to room temperature on the benchtop for 8 hours

steps.title: In a 6-well tissue culture plate,...
steps.instruction: In a 6-well tissue culture plate, prepare the following rehydration series (2 mL per well): 100% ethanol (×2), 95% ethanol (×2), 75% ethanol, 50% ethanol, 25% ethanol, and 1× PBS

steps.title: Transfer planchet assemblies sequentially through each...
steps.instruction: Transfer planchet assemblies sequentially through each ethanol step and then into PBS. Incubate approximately 5–10 minutes per step.

steps.title: Sample C. elegans (dextran cryoprotectant)
steps.instruction: Sample C. elegans (dextran cryoprotectant): Animals typically detach from the planchet and enter suspension around the 50% ethanol step. By the PBS wash, animals are freely suspended and can be collected bypipette.

steps.title: Sample Tardigrades (FBS cryoprotectant)
steps.instruction: Sample Tardigrades (FBS cryoprotectant): FBS forms a gel upon hydration, retaining animals within the planchet cavity. Tardigrades must be manually extracted from the gel using fine forceps under the dissection microscope, after the PBS wash.

steps.title: Wash specimens twice in 0
steps.instruction: Wash specimens twice in 0.1 M NaHCO3 using the spot plate (2 × 5 min).

steps.title: Incubate specimens in 0
steps.instruction: Incubate specimens in 0.1% (v/v) glycidyl methacrylate (GMA) in 0.1 M NaHCO3 overnight shaking at room temperature. GMA covalently crosslinks amine groups on proteins and will act as the anchor to the acrylate backbone of the expansion gel.

steps.title: Wash specimens 3× in 1× PBS (5 min each)
steps.instruction: Wash specimens 3× in 1× PBS (5 min each).

steps.title: Transfer specimens to non-activated monomer solution
steps.instruction: Transfer specimens to non-activated monomer solution: 23% (w/v) sodium acrylate, 10% (w/v) acrylamide, 0.1% (w/v) Bis, in 1× PBS.

steps.title: Incubate overnight at 4°C with gentle...
steps.instruction: Incubate overnight at 4°C with gentle agitation to allow full infiltration.

steps.title: Prepare the gelation chamber
steps.instruction: Prepare the gelation chamber: place two layers of double-sided adhesive tape (~200 µm total spacer height) onto a clean glass slide as parallel walls.

steps.title: Remove as much non-activated monomer solution...
steps.instruction: Remove as much non-activated monomer solution from the specimens as possible without drying the sample.

steps.title: Prepare activated monomer solution by adding...
steps.instruction: Prepare activated monomer solution by adding TEMED and APS to fresh cold non-activated monomer solution to final concentrations of 0.25% each. Mix by gentle pipetting.

steps.title: Pipette the activated monomer solution onto...
steps.instruction: Pipette the activated monomer solution onto the specimens and mix briefly by pipetting up and down. Immediately transfer the specimen + solution into the gelation chamber on the glass slide. Place acoverslip gently on top.

steps.title: Transfer the assembly to a sealed,...
steps.instruction: Transfer the assembly to a sealed, humidified chamber (plastic container with damp tissue). Incubate at 37°C for 1 hour.

steps.title: After gelation, carefully disassemble the gelation...
steps.instruction: After gelation, carefully disassemble the gelation chamber and cut the gel piece containing the embedded specimen away from surrounding gel using a clean razor blade or scalpel.

steps.title: Transfer the gel piece containing the...
steps.instruction: Transfer the gel piece containing the specimen into enzymatic disruption buffer: 1 mg/mL collagenase Type IV, 1 mg/mL chitinase, 500 mM NaCl, 50 mM CaCl2, in Tris-HCl pH 7.0.

steps.title: Incubate at 37°C with shaking overnight
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37°C with shaking overnight. For this I used a VWR Incubating Microplate Shaker, set to 100 RPM.

steps.title: Transfer gel piece to SDS denaturation...
steps.instruction: Transfer gel piece to SDS denaturation solution (200 mM SDS, 200 mM NaCl, 50 mM Tris, pH 9.0). Incubate at 95°C for 2 hours.

steps.title: Wash gel piece 2–3× in deionized...
steps.instruction: Wash gel piece 2–3× in deionized water to expand and remove the SDS.

steps.title: Equilibrate expanded gel briefly in PBS until itssize change halts
steps.instruction: Equilibrate expanded gel briefly in PBS until itssize change halts.

steps.title: Incubate in 20 µM NHS ester...
steps.instruction: Incubate in 20 µM NHS ester dye in PBS for 2 hours at room temperature with gentle agitation.

steps.title: Wash and re-expand in deionized water (3× water exchanges)
steps.instruction: Wash and re-expand in deionized water (3× water exchanges).

steps.title: Equilibrate gel in PBS + 3% BSA + 0
steps.instruction: Equilibrate gel in PBS + 3% BSA + 0.1% sodium azide.

steps.title: Incubate with primary antibody (1:100 dilution)...
steps.instruction: Incubate with primary antibody (1:100 dilution) in PBS + 3% BSA + 0.1% sodium azide, overnight at 37°C. The sodium azide at this stage is used to prevent any bacterial growth in the solution during the incubation. As the sample is fixed and attached to the gel, there should be no change to organelle morphology.

steps.title: Wash 3× in PBS (5 min each)
steps.instruction: Wash 3× in PBS (5 min each).

steps.title: Incubate with secondary antibody (5 µg/mL)...
steps.instruction: Incubate with secondary antibody (5 µg/mL) in PBS + 3% BSA + 0.1% sodium azide, overnight at 37°C.

steps.title: Wash 2× in PBS (5 min each)
steps.instruction: Wash 2× in PBS (5 min each).

steps.title: Post-fix in 6% paraformaldehyde (PFA) in...
steps.instruction: Post-fix in 6% paraformaldehyde (PFA) in PBS for 30 minutes at room temperature. This step is to retain the antibody staining. If omitted, the antibody signal will decrease significantly over the course of days. Omit this step if one plans on doing sequential labeling, as the PFA will negatively impact the sample antigenicity for further labeling.

steps.title: After expansion, incubate gel in 1:10,000...
steps.instruction: After expansion, incubate gel in 1:10,000 SYBR Gold in deionized water for 10 minutes.

steps.title: Wash out excess stain with 2–3 changes of deionized water
steps.instruction: Wash out excess stain with 2–3 changes of deionized water.

steps.title: Screening
steps.instruction: Screening : Place the expanded gel on an uncoated glass-bottom MatTek dish. Examine on a widefield fluorescence microscope using a water immersion objective to identify which face of the gel contains the most specimens closest to the coverslip surface. Confirm sample quality and select regions of interest. In this experiment I had 10 specimens per gel, and from these I would have 3 to 5 that had the majority of the sample imageable by high NA water immersion objectives, and all would be imageable by water dipping objectives.

steps.title: Once the preferred face is identified,...
steps.instruction: Once the preferred face is identified, place the gel flat on a poly-D-lysine (PDL)-coated 25 mm round coverslip with the specimen-rich face down (against the PDL surface).

steps.title: Remove excess water gently with a...
steps.instruction: Remove excess water gently with a Kimwipe or lint-free tissue by blotting around the edge of the gel. Do not allow the gel to dry.

steps.title: Gently press the gel against the...
steps.instruction: Gently press the gel against the coverslip using the flat of a clean fine-bristled paint brush to promote adhesion.

steps.title: Submerge the coverslip and gel in...
steps.instruction: Submerge the coverslip and gel in deionized water in the well of a 6-well plate for ~30 minutes to allow the gel to re-equilibrate.

steps.title: Mount the PDL-coated coverslip with adherent...
steps.instruction: Mount the PDL-coated coverslip with adherent gel into a coverslip holder. Fill with the same deionized water used to store the sample as immersion medium.

steps.title: Image using one of the following platforms:
steps.instruction: Image using one of the following platforms:

steps.title: Nikon NSPARC array detector system
steps.instruction: Nikon NSPARC array detector system: for high-sensitivity, enhanced-resolution confocal imaging.

steps.title: Yokogawa CSU-W1 spinning disk confocal
steps.instruction: Yokogawa CSU-W1 spinning disk confocal: for rapid volumetric acquisition.

steps.title: Acquire image channels appropriate to the labeling
steps.instruction: Acquire image channels appropriate to the labeling: NHS ester (total-protein morphology), antibody (specific targets), and/or SYBR Gold (DNA/nuclei).

steps.title: Transfer all planchets to a cryogenic...
steps.instruction: Transfer all planchets to a cryogenic storage vessel and transport to the laboratory immersed LN2.
